chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.596 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.3141/2026 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.3142/2026 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 24.02.2026 Mr. Saadi Sardar, advocate for applicant ------------------------------------ 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. Applicant/accused Subhan Atif son of Atif Naseer Bhatti, who is presently in Baku, Azerbaijan, has filed instant criminal bail application through his attorney/father Atif Naseer Bhatti, seeking protective-cum-transitory bail in FIR No.371/2022, registered at P.S. FIA, AHT Circle, Karachi for offence under Sections 419, 420, 468, 471, 109, PPC. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicant he is innocent and has been falsely involved in this case for mala fide intentions and ulterior motives; the he was unaware about registration of above FIR; that applicant is a law abiding citizen and intends to surrender before the concerned Court but he apprehends his arrest at the hands of concerned police in order to humiliate and disgrace, hence he needs protection in order to enable him to appear before the concerned Court. Without touching the merits of the case, protective-cum- transitory bail is granted to applicant above named in the aforesaid FIR, for a period of ten days from today, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicant is directed to surrender before the concerned Court in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith.

Without touching the merits of the case, protective-cum- transitory bail is granted to applicant above named in the aforesaid FIR, for a period of ten days from today, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Applicant is directed to surrender before the concerned Court in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of ten days from today or whenever the applicant surrenders before the concerned Court, which one occurs earlier. The applicant is cautioned that this concession of protective bail will not be extended for any reason whatsoever and if he fails to surrender himself before the relevant Court during validity of this protective bail, his bail bond shall stand forfeited. Instant Criminal Bail Application stands disposed of in the foregoing terms. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
